transcript.text:
[3 bibim noodles, 2 regular noodles, 2 tteokboki, 2 tangsuyuk, 1 yaki mandu]
14,000 Won (~$11.69 USD)
[Total number of 10 dishes]
[10 servings for just 14,000 Won...?]
[Very cheap eats, but how will it taste?]
Delicious
A lot of students come

Quite amazing
[Sales going up]
[She's eating that by herself...?]
[Hey, not bad]
[One of the most low-priced mukbang starting now]
Hello everyone
I'm here at Uijeongbu today
I used to come here a lot in my early 20s

And there's this 1 spot that I went often
Tangsuyuk (sweet & sour pork) was 1,500 Won (~$1.25 USD)
And noodles were at 1,000 Won (~$0.84 USD)
It's still here, so I had to come back
It's this one over here
Tasuyuk price went up by 500 Won
But noodles are still 1,000 Won
How do they even make money?
Let's head inside
[Honey Don] [Uijeongbu, Gyeonggi-do]
Hello
The prices are not too different from 5 years ago
That's when I last came here
Everything seems the same except for Tangsuyuk
You have to order upfront Let's go
3 bibim noodles (sweet & spicy),

2 regular noodles (anchovy broth),
2 tteokboki (spicy rice cake),
2 tangsuyuk (sweet & sour pork),
1 yaki mandu (fried dumplings)
14,000 Won (~$11.69 USD)
[She just ordered 10 servings of food]
[And she looks very confused]
[We'll show you 1,000 Won noodles]
[Seasoning sauce, kimchi, and dried seaweed]
[Anchovy broth noodles]
[Tteokboki (spicy rice cake)]
[Freshly deep-fried tangsuyuk and yaki mandu]
[All for only 14,000 Won! (~$11.69 USD)]
It's been a while
I didn't order this much back then, but I still ordered a good amount
Bon appetit

Let's mix this up first
The portions are a bit small
But the prices are 1,000~1,500 Won
Perfect for small eaters
And it's a quick and simple way to take care of a meal
I used to come here a lot when I had to quickly come and go
Even if it was for 5~6 dishes
Delicious
A sweet & spicy flavor that everyone likes
It has kimchi too
[Where did the noodles go?]
I used to eat the tangsuyuk a lot
The price level in the economy went up in general in the past 5 years
The portions got a little smaller too
It's still considered cheap though
I couldn't remember how it tasted

Now I remember it being tasty
So good
Sweet
Chewy and tasty
Yummy
[1 bite with lots of sauce on it]
It's good, but I'm gonna need like 10 of these
10?
20?
Tteokboki...
I used to eat this a lot from here too
Flour rice cake
Just like the ones that are sold in front of schools
Tasty
Anchovy broth noodles It's only 1,000 Won (~$0.84 USD)
I think this has to be the one that's most cost-efficient

Delicious
It's cheap And it's something that everyone would enjoy
This is really worth it
It has soup, so it fills you up more
And it's cheap
Yaki mandu

Freshly fried
Tasty

3 pieces for 1,000 Won (~$0.84 USD)
10 dishes for just 14,000 Won?
Doesn't make sense
For normal people,
1 tteokboki, 1 tangsuyuk, and 1 bowl of noodles should fill you up
[And even that comes out to be 4,500 Won (~$3.76 USD)]

A lot of students probably come here a lot
[Scrape every up!]
There's no way
She's small too
[A walking mystery of the human body]
It seems like regular customers order 2 dishes
2 of the most expensive items here only comes out to 3,500 Won

[1 bowl of noodles gone in 5 seconds]
Sodas normally cost 2,000 Won or even 3,000 Won at restaurants
But this was 1,000 Won
Do they even make any money?
A lot of students come to our store
It may seem empty right now because of covid, but we have student regulars

Quite amazing...

Mandu
[Dipped in tteokboki sauce]

[That looks good...]
It's so crispy

She finished it

It's spicy
Yaki mandu tastes really good
I think the mandu is really cost-efficient

That's incredible

But what if she gets indigestion?
